title: 10 possible side effects of Ocimum tenuiflorum (Holy Basil) if used improperly
intro: Ocimum tenuiflorum, also known as holy basil, is a plant that is often used in traditional medicine, but if used improperly, it can have negative health effects. First, it can cause digestive issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea if consumed in large amounts. Additionally, it may cause low blood sugar levels and should be used with caution by those with diabetes. Moreover, it can have effects on the reproductive system and hormonal balance, and therefore, it is not recommended for pregnant women or those who are breastfeeding. Lastly, it can interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and sedatives, and thus, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using holy basil if you are taking any medication.
